title: fix(gen-image): store LB latency under its own KV key
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
Follow-up to #11869. The latency weight (`lastMs`) was being wiped ~29% of the time in production — the fast 4090 kept dropping to the neutral default and getting under-weighted.

- **Cause:** heartbeats (`registerServer`) and latency samples (`recordLatency`) wrote the *same* KV key. A stale heartbeat read would write back an entry without `lastMs`, clobbering it.
- **Fix:** store latency under a separate key (`image:latency:...`) that only `recordLatency` writes. Heartbeat writes only the liveness entry — neither writer can touch the other's key, so `lastMs` can never be wiped.
- `getRegisteredServers` merges the latency key into each entry.
- Simplifies both writers (removes the read-modify-write from each).
- Adds a test proving `lastMs` survives a re-registering heartbeat.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

gist.headline: Fast lane restored
gist.blurb: Fixed a load balancing bug that was occasionally forgetting how fast our best GPUs are. Image generation routing is back to peak efficiency.
gist.summary: Separated server heartbeat and latency tracking into distinct KV keys to prevent race conditions from wiping latency weights.
gist.impact: Image generation requests will route more reliably to the fastest available GPUs, like the 4090s, improving overall response times.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee acting as a traffic director, routing glowing data packets to a fast server. Represents the fixed load balancer routing requests to the fastest GPUs.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly the bee wearing a tiny traffic cop hat, directing glowing data packets onto a high-speed neon track. A chunky retro server monitor glows brightly in the background. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley, chunky sprites.
